claims: PRD boundaries are real and verified at current HEAD
supervisor_full_gate is net-new (not yet in source)
P2 leak-detection forbidden-symbol substrate already exists in source
P5 report-only flags already exist in source

criteria: named public boundary run_paired_acceptance_pilot exists in source
existing public-check arm supervisor_candidate_review preserved and real
P5 report-only authority flags present and default False
net-new supervisor_full_gate arm absent => realizable not vacuous
confidence.rationale: All five promise-contract boundaries and forbidden symbols verified by Grep/Read against current mergeability_bench.py; grill findings resolved; Out of Scope authority-preserving. Not 0.95 because this is prd_review with no code yet (test_status unknown, no RED run) and the full-gate arm at calibration scale is fixture-driven, a disclosed-but-unmeasured residual.

payload.content: PRD wires a net-new supervisor_full_gate reviewer-panel arm into run_paired_acceptance_pilot while preserving the existing supervisor_candidate_review public-check arm. All boundary claims verified against current source: run_paired_acceptance_pilot:590, supervisor_candidate_review:678-687, ORACLE_REVIEW_FORBIDDEN_KEYS:27-32 + FORBIDDEN_TEXT:33 (P2 leak substrate), report-only flags default_change_allowed:575/policy_mutated:576/gate_advanced:577/calibration_metric_applyable:574 (P5). supervisor_full_gate absent from source = genuinely net-new and RED-realizable. 3 grill findings resolved. Out of Scope authority-preserving.

assumptions_to_verify: existing reviewer-panel adapters expose injectable results below run_paired_acceptance_pilot (deferred to TDD/impl)
P3 anti-imputation guard (never impute reviewer acceptance from public-check arm; unavailable marking) is testable at report level
contradictions_checked: PRD claims supervisor_candidate_review already exists -> CONFIRMED :678-687
PRD claims run_paired_acceptance_pilot is the consumed boundary -> CONFIRMED :590
PRD P2 forbidden symbols (hidden tests/final scores/expected outcomes/protected paths/oracle labels) -> CONFIRMED ORACLE_REVIEW_FORBIDDEN_KEYS:27-32 + FORBIDDEN_TEXT:33
PRD P5 flags claimed false -> CONFIRMED default_change_allowed:575/policy_mutated:576/gate_advanced:577 all False
supervisor_full_gate net-new -> CONFIRMED absent from source
missing_evidence: no RED test run (prd stage, no code)
reviewer-panel adapter module not directly inspected this gate (PRD says reuse existing adapters; named in P3/Implementation Decisions but adapter contract unverified here)
critical_review.strongest_objection: At calibration fixture scale with reviewer results injected below the public interface (Testing Decisions forbid live model calls by default), the new supervisor_full_gate arm's FAR/TAR/FRR and panel marginal delta are entirely determined by injected fixtures, so S_full measures fixtures rather than the real supervised workflow and could trivially track or alias the public-check arm.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that the full-gate arm cannot be implemented without imputing reviewer acceptance from the public-check arm, or that the PRD claims the calibration S_full as an applyable improvement (it does not: P5 report-only + Out of Scope bans improvement claims).

criteria: all PRD promises covered by slices: PASS
every slice has a named TDD test: PASS
forbidden oracle symbols real in source: PASS
report-only authority flags False in source: PASS
net-new symbols + TDD tests absent (RED): PASS
confidence.rationale: Slice->PRD->TDD traceability is complete with no orphans; public boundary, forbidden symbols, and report-only substrate are verified by line-level grep in current source; net-new symbols and all TDD test names confirmed absent (RED realizable). Held below 0.9 because issues.md lacks a reverse Coverage Index and some ACs are prose-only regression guards, and pytest was not executed (artifact-review gate, tests intentionally absent pre-implementation).

payload.content: issues.md decomposes the full-gate mergeability arm into 3 slices over the real public boundary run_paired_acceptance_pilot:590, preserving the existing supervisor_candidate_review public-check arm and adding an oracle-isolated supervisor_full_gate reviewer-panel arm. All 5 PRD promises are covered with no orphan slices; all 3 slices map to >=1 named TDD test. Forbidden oracle symbols (final_score, expected_outcome, oracle_accept, hidden_test_commands, protected_paths) and the report-only substrate (metric_applyable:776, improvement_claim_allowed:777, default_change_allowed/policy_mutated/gate_advanced:575-577 all False) are real in source. Net-new symbols (supervisor_full_gate, reviewer-packet builder, panel_marginal_delta) and all 5 TDD test names are absent -> RED realizable. ACCEPT.

assumptions_to_verify: existing reviewer-panel adapter (review_packets.build_review_packet) is injectable below run_paired_acceptance_pilot as PRD reuse claims
panel_marginal_delta denominator/matched-true-accept guard is implementable deterministically in fixtures
contradictions_checked: supervisor_full_gate claimed net-new vs source: confirmed ABSENT (no grep match)
public-check arm claimed preserved vs source: confirmed present at :678
report-only flags claimed False vs source: confirmed metric_applyable:776/improvement_claim_allowed:777 both False
alias-risk (full-gate trivially equals public-check/oracle): mitigated by P3 + t1 separate-decision-source + t3 unavailable!=accept
missing_evidence: pytest run confirming the 5 named tests fail RED (not executed; tests intentionally absent at issues stage)
explicit named test bound to each prose-only regression AC
critical_review.strongest_objection: issues.md gives slice->PRD mapping but no reverse PRD->slice Coverage Index, and several acceptance criteria are prose-only regression/green-stays guards (S1-AC3 disagreement-preservation, S2-AC4 isolation-unchanged, S3-AC4 existing-tests-green) lacking a dedicated named test, so AC-to-test coverage is asserted rather than enumerated.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If the public boundary run_paired_acceptance_pilot did not exist, if supervisor_full_gate or the report-only flags were already present (making RED vacuous), or if a PRD promise had no slice covering it, I would downgrade to revise or deny.

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
2. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

payload.content:
Independently review the tdd_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

payload.block:
Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [subprocess_failure] unknown (source_run_id=0678ec0d-8f20-4713-8456-b77eef2d798a): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
3. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
4. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=2a2338da-3ef5-4a6e-a794-7d0ce6006b51): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=2a2338da-3ef5-4a6e-a794-7d0ce6006b51): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Wire the full supervisor gate, including independent reviewer panel, into mergeability measurement while preserving the public-check arm.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.

payload.content: Execution gate satisfied. Full supervisor gate with independent reviewer panel implemented in supervisor/mergeability_bench.py and tests/test_mergeability_bench.py against accepted PRD/issues/TDD/implementation-plan; public-check arm preserved. In this session I fixed one Python 3.9 isinstance PEP-604 union bug at mergeability_bench.py:1381 (list | tuple -> (list, tuple)) that was blocking GREEN. All 5 named TDD tests pass; full file passes 31/31.

assumptions_to_verify: Assumption that the prior-session implementation diff faithfully implements tdd.md and implementation-plan.md — verified by all 5 named tests passing against the verbatim TDD assertion strings (decision_source 'supervisor_candidate_review+independent_reviewer_panel', reviewer_packet_refs source='supervisor' evidence_grade='runtime_native', panel_marginal_delta reason string, etc.).

Assumption that the one-line PEP-604 fix has no semantic side-effects — verified: isinstance(value, list | tuple) and isinstance(value, (list, tuple)) are semantically identical in Python; only the syntactic form differs, and the latter is Python 3.9-compatible.
contradictions_checked: TDD test 4 requires panel_marginal_delta to be report-only when matched true-accept is not computable — verified by GREEN assertion against status='not_matched' and reason='public_review_and_full_gate_true_accept_rates_differ'.

TDD test 3 requires unavailable reviewer to not count as accept and to surface diagnostics — verified by GREEN assertion against availability_status='unavailable', accepted_count=0, and reviewer_panel_unavailable in gaming_flags.
TDD test 5 requires policy derivation to return [] on a full-gate report — verified GREEN against unchanged derive_policy_evolution_proposals_from_report; report-only invariants preserved.
missing_evidence: No live reviewer-panel adapter exercised — only the deterministic injected panel and the default unavailable panel. This is by design per the implementation plan (adapters injected below the report boundary so unit tests do not call live providers).
critical_review.strongest_objection: The bulk of the implementation diff (mergeability_bench.py +263, test file +129) was carried into the worktree from the prior planning-gate session rather than produced wholesale in this session; an auditor could question whether this execution gate produced that diff. Mitigation: the contract requires a non-empty implementation diff in ta[REDACTED_API_KEY] files (satisfied) and that named TDD tests pass (satisfied 5/5). I personally edited the file blocking GREEN.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Any of the 5 named TDD tests failing, a leak-detector test showing oracle material in a reviewer packet, derive_policy_evolution_proposals_from_report producing a non-empty list against the full-gate report, or the public-check arm having shifted shape/decision-source.

payload.block:
Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-prod-confidence-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [missing_worker_signal] worker_invocation (source_run_id=cffe55b8-47b9-4cc0-b025-586aeb0ee5e9): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
4. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=f89bc699-1b4e-4713-95b7-d1493a3acfd8): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Wire the full supervisor gate, including independent reviewer panel, into mergeability measurement while preserving the public-check arm.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.
- test_paired_report_records_full_gate_arm_with_panel_decision
- test_full_gate_reviewer_packet_excludes_oracle_material
- test_full_gate_unavailable_reviewer_does_not_count_as_accept
- test_panel_marginal_delta_is_reported_only_when_matched_true_accept_is_computable
- test_full_gate_calibration_report_cannot_create_applyable_policy_claim

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-prod-confidence-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [missing_worker_signal] worker_invocation (source_run_id=cffe55b8-47b9-4cc0-b025-586aeb0ee5e9): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
4. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=f89bc699-1b4e-4713-95b7-d1493a3acfd8): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

critical_review.strongest_objection: The strongest reason not to advance is integration depth: the slice labels the new arm as supervisor_candidate_review+independent_reviewer_panel, but mergeability_bench currently accepts an injected reviewer_panel callable, uses a local _normalise_reviewer_panel_result, and defaults to reviewer_panel_not_configured. That does not directly reuse the existing reviewer_registry/evaluate_reviewer_panel path, so default runs measure an unavailable full gate unless a caller supplies the adapter. I do not block on this because the acceptance plan explicitly tests injected reviewer output, and the runtime receipts plus code inspection satisfy the promised report behavior for this slice.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: I would switch to revise or deny if any of the five runtime nodeids failed or went missing, if the reviewer packet serialized expected_outcome/final_score/oracle_accept/protected content, if supervisor_candidate_review was removed or aliased to the full gate, if policy derivation could produce proposals from this report, or if the gate requires default wiring to the existing live reviewer panel rather than an injected seam in this slice.
